'Bhooth Bangla' opens to strong Rs 23 crore worldwide debut; ends 'Dhurandhar 2' reign
description: Akshay Kumar's 'Bhooth Bangla' has stormed the box office, securing a massive Rs 23.90 crore worldwide debut on its opening day. This impressive performance has dethroned Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' after its four-week reign. The horror-comedy's strong opening positions it among Akshay Kumar's top post-pandemic releases.

'Fake' caste certificate row: AIMIM corporator Sahar Sheikh’s father denies charges
description: The father of AIMIM corporator Sahar Sheikh has refuted allegations of a fake caste certificate, calling the controversy a politically motivated stunt. Yunus Sheikh stated his daughter is being targeted due to her political growth and promised to provide authentic documents to counter the claims. He also defended Sahar's attendance at an IPL match, dismissing public criticism.

title: At site where father works as guard, 7-year-old girl kidnapped and raped in Ghaziabad
description: A seven-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped and sexually assaulted while sleeping at a Ghaziabad construction site.  CCTV footage shows the child with an unidentified suspect.  The family, originally from Madhya Pradesh, reported the incident, and police are actively working to identify and apprehend the perpetrator. This marks the third such case involving a minor in the district recently.

title: American Express executive found dead in Gurgaon; wife finds body inside flat
description: A 33-year-old American Express executive was found dead by suicide in his Gurgaon flat on Friday. His wife, who flew in from Mumbai after he stopped answering calls following a fight, discovered the scene. The couple, married four months ago, faced marital strain due to their differing work locations. Police are investigating the incident, noting the deceased was under stress.

title: Sukesh Chandrashekhar case: Jacqueline Fernandez seeks to turn approver
description: A Delhi court has asked the Enforcement Directorate to respond to a plea by actor Jacqueline Fernandez to become a witness in a Rs 200 crore money laundering case. Fernandez is accused of receiving gifts worth up to Rs 7 crore from alleged conman Sukesh Chandrashekhar, a charge she denies.

title: TCS Nashik case: Cops reach Mumbai in search of TCS staffer, question her husband
description: Nashik Crime Branch is searching for a 26-year-old TCS employee in Mumbra, Thane, accused of religious harassment. Her husband, questioned by police, stated she returned to Nashik. The woman, reportedly pregnant, is seeking anticipatory bail. The National Commission for Women has formed a fact-finding committee to investigate the allegations.

title: Delhi sees 3 seasons in a day: Hazy start, mercury hits 41°C, pounding rain
description: Delhi experienced its hottest day of the season at 41 degrees Celsius on Friday, only to be unexpectedly cooled by intense thunderstorms and heavy rain. The sudden downpour caught residents and the IMD off guard, causing significant traffic disruptions across the city. Forecasters anticipate continued high temperatures over the weekend.

description: FSSAI has issued a stern warning against unsafe fruit ripening practices, directing states to strictly enforce regulations against prohibited artificial agents like calcium carbide. The advisory highlights serious health risks associated with these chemicals and mandates intensified market surveillance and legal action against offenders.

Microsoft’s & Apple’s coding tools will be ‘dead soon,': Creator of Antrophic's AI coding tool
description: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, predicts traditional coding tools like VS Code and Xcode will soon be obsolete. He believes AI agents will soon handle entire development workflows, rendering current IDEs unnecessary. This shift will redefine software engineers into 'builders' who orchestrate AI, requiring adaptability and early experimentation from professionals.
